lead: Exploring the intersection of AI and health, this brief highlights the latest developments in health technology, legal implications, and emerging health-focused wearables.

stories.title: Using AI for health questions? Here are 4 tips for the most accurate answers.
stories.summary: As AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude gain popularity for health inquiries, users are advised to follow specific prompting techniques to ensure accurate responses. The article outlines four practical tips to enhance the reliability of AI-generated health information, emphasizing the importance of context and specificity in queries.

stories.title: Health AI and the law: Could your chatbot doc testify against you?
stories.summary: The legal landscape surrounding health-focused AI remains uncertain, particularly regarding user privacy and the potential for chatbots to be compelled to disclose user interactions in court. This article explores the implications of these legal challenges and the need for clearer regulations to protect users.

stories.title: AI can screen 15 million molecules in a day. It still can’t cure Alzheimer’s.
stories.summary: Despite the impressive capabilities of AI in drug discovery, significant challenges remain in developing effective treatments for complex diseases like Alzheimer's. This article discusses the gap between AI's potential and its current limitations in addressing major health issues.

stories.title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
stories.summary: Onix is launching a platform that offers AI-generated health advice from digital replicas of wellness influencers. While this innovation aims to make expert advice more accessible, it raises questions about the value and reliability of AI in personal health management.

stories.title: Google Is Changing How Gemini Handles a User’s Mental Health Crisis
stories.summary: Google's Gemini is set to implement significant changes in how it responds to users experiencing mental health crises. These updates aim to enhance user safety and provide more appropriate support, reflecting the growing recognition of mental health in tech applications.

stories.title: I walked 3,000 steps with my Apple Watch, Google Pixel, and Oura Ring - this tracker was most accurate
stories.summary: A comparative study of step-tracking accuracy among popular health devices reveals significant differences in performance. The findings highlight the importance of choosing the right wearable for fitness tracking, particularly for those focused on health metrics.

stories.title: Meta’s New AI Asked for My Raw Health Data—and Gave Me Terrible Advice
stories.summary: A user recounts their experience with Meta's AI health assistant, which requested sensitive health data but ultimately provided poor advice. This raises concerns about the reliability of AI in health contexts and the potential risks of sharing personal information.

stories.title: Dutch healthcare software vendor goes dark after ransomware attack
stories.summary: A ransomware attack has left a Dutch healthcare software vendor incapacitated, highlighting the vulnerabilities of digital health systems. This incident underscores the critical need for robust cybersecurity measures in healthcare technology to protect sensitive patient data.
